_comment: Cohere ASR per-tensor tolerances for compare_tensors.py.
Reference: MLX (mlx-audio) running fp32 inference on Apple Silicon.
C++: ggml CPU fp32 compute, weights stored as bf16 in GGUF.
Root cause of early-layer max_abs: the mel STFT uses fp64 in C++
vs fp32 in MLX. This produces ~0.34 max_abs in the top mel bins
(124-127), affecting 0.43% of elements. The pre_encode conv layers
amplify these sparse outliers (max_abs 8.7) but mean_abs stays low
(0.032). The errors attenuate through the 48-layer conformer:
dec.logits uses inf tolerance because log(softmax(x)) produces
-inf at different positions when weights differ slightly. Validated
